rationale: The citing paper’s stated research gap explicitly builds on “prior feature-fatigue findings” and seeks to explain the initial attraction to feature-rich products, which directly aligns with the cited paper’s core contribution introducing the feature fatigue effect. The two papers have highly overlapping constructs (feature-rich vs feature-poor choice, capability vs usability before/after use), and the cited work appears to be a foundational prior result rather than a generic mention. Given this direct conceptual dependence, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
